title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle threw seven scoreless innings against the Yankees, allowing just one hit and striking out seven.
Tolle, battling an illness, carried a perfect game into the sixth inning before conceding the first hit.
Tolle has a 2.78 ERA over 12 starts this season, excelling particularly against the Yankees with 18 strikeouts in two games.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Celtics could consider trading Derrick White and Baylor Scheierman for Trey Murphy and Yves Missi.
The team is exploring center options, including Mitchell Robinson and Zach Collins, to improve offensive depth.
Celtics may offload Sam Hauser's $10 million contract for roster flexibility and pursue LeBron James at a discount.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins acquired JJ Peterka from Utah Mammoth in exchange for two first-round picks, including Boston's 2026 selection.
Peterka, known for his speed and scoring ability, has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three NHL seasons.
JJ Peterka, from Munich, Germany, has built a hockey rink in his backyard to promote the sport in Bavaria.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics are considering trade offers for Jaylen Brown, targeting at least four first-round picks.
Portland Trail Blazers, with draft capital and young talent, show interest in trading for Jaylen Brown.
Detroit Pistons might pursue a sign-and-trade involving Jalen Duren for Jaylen Brown in free agency.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' (2025) is now streaming on Disney+, featuring Oona Chaplin as Varang.
James Gunn's 'Superman' (2025), starring David Corenswet and Rachel Brosnahan, is streaming on HBO Max.
'Avatar: The Last Airbender' Season 2's live-action on Netflix expands the journey to the city of Ba Sing Se.

title: Red Sox place Marcelo Mayer on 10-day IL with forearm issue
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox player Marcelo Mayer placed on 10-day IL with a forearm bone stress reaction.
Tsung-Che Cheng to debut at shortstop for Boston in Mayer's absence against the Yankees.
Boston Red Sox struggling with multiple injuries, including Mayer, Roman Anthony, and Trevor Story.

title: Bruins reportedly trade for Utah forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are trading two first-round picks to acquire Utah Mammoth forward JJ Peterka.
Peterka, 24, underperformed in Utah with 47 points but is signed until the 2029-30 season at $7.7M annually.
Peterka could serve as a replacement for Viktor Arvidsson and continues the Bruins' trend of recruiting German-born players.

title: Craig Breslow on his job security, whether the Red Sox will buy or sell, and more
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox chief baseball officer Craig Breslow believes the team can still improve despite being last in the American League East.
Breslow acknowledges potential changes, facing trade deadline decisions with attractive sellable assets like Sonny Gray and Willson Contreras.
Breslow emphasized focus on job responsibilities, disregarding job security discussions with owner John Henry.

title: Review: ‘Supergirl’ is a hero in search of a better movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Actress Milly Alcock stars as Supergirl, but the film struggles with a bland and derivative plot.
Director Craig Gillespie and screenwriter Ana Nogueira attempt comedic elements with limited success.
Flashbacks explore Supergirl's upbringing, revealing her trauma, but lack emotional impact in the storyline.

title: One of Cambridge’s most beloved Japanese restaurants is closing after 34 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Tampopo, a Japanese restaurant in Cambridge's Porter Exchange, is closing after 34 years due to owner Yasumasa Ito's retirement.
The restaurant, located at 1815 Massachusetts Ave., will serve its final meal on June 30, impacting generations of customers.
Former employee Kotaro Morita flew from Japan to assist during Tampopo's final weeks, while customers share memories online.
